req.title: Search patients (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall search patients by name, MRN, or DOB; only patients in their care shall be returned. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-088 requires: (1) clinicians can search patients by name/MRN/DOB, (2) only patients in their care are returned, (3) an AuditEvent is emitted per operation, (4) malformed queries return 400. Five tests are designed: EP-positive (valid name search returns care-scoped bundle + audit), NEG (malformed query returns 400), BVA (single-character name at minimum boundary), EP-negative corrected from STC (patient outside clinician's care is excluded — this is a scoping/access-control negative path best classified as EP with kind=negative, testing the equivalence partition of 'patient not in care'), and DT (multi-param search combining name+DOB). The violation was test[3] having kind=negative but heuristic=STC; STC is reserved for state-transition coverage and is inherently a positive structural heuristic. The fix is to change heuristic to EP (the out-of-care exclusion is a distinct equivalence partition of the care-scoping rule) which is valid for a negative kind test.

tests.name: test_search_patient_by_name_returns_scoped_bundle_and_audit
preconditions: At least one patient assigned to this clinician exists with a known family name
AuditEvent log is accessible via /AuditEvent endpoint
expected: HTTP 200 returned
Bundle resourceType is 'Bundle'
All entries in bundle belong to clinician's care list
At least one AuditEvent recorded for this search operation
AuditEvent.action is 'E' (execute/query)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_search_patient_by_name_returns_scoped_bundle_and_audit(
    clinician_auth_headers, assigned_patient, fhir_client, audit_poller
):
    family = assigned_patient["name"][0]["family"]
    r = fhir_client.get("/Patient", params={"name": family}, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert r.status_code == 200
    bundle = r.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1
    care_ids = {p["id"] for p in assigned_patient["care_list"]}
    for entry in entries:
        pid = entry["resource"]["id"]
        assert pid in care_ids, f"{pid} not in clinician care list"
    audit = audit_poller("/AuditEvent", entity_type="Patient", action="E")
    assert len(audit) >= 1
    assert audit[0]["action"] == "E"

tests.name: test_search_patient_malformed_query_returns_400
actions: GET /Patient?name= (empty name param) with clinician auth header
expected: HTTP 400 returned for empty name
HTTP 400 returned for invalid date string
HTTP 400 returned for invalid MRN characters
Response body contains OperationOutcome resourceType for each case
OperationOutcome.issue[0].severity is 'error' for each case
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("params,label", [
    ({"name": ""}, "empty_name"),
    ({"birthdate": "not-a-date"}, "invalid_dob"),
    ({"identifier": "!!INVALID!!"}, "invalid_mrn_chars"),
])
def test_search_patient_malformed_query_returns_400(
    clinician_auth_headers, fhir_client, params, label
):
    r = fhir_client.get("/Patient", params=params, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert r.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400 for {label}, got {r.status_code}"
    body = r.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i["severity"] == "error" for i in issues)

tests.name: test_search_patient_single_char_name_boundary
preconditions: A patient whose family name starts with 'A' is assigned to the clinician
fhir-lite supports prefix-match on name parameter
actions: GET /Patient?name=A with clinician auth header (single-character boundary input)
Record HTTP status and bundle entry count
expected: HTTP 200 returned (single-char is valid minimum input)
Bundle resourceType is 'Bundle'
All returned patients belong to clinician's care
AuditEvent emitted for this query
trace: R-HLT-088: BVA min-length boundary for search term

tests.name: test_search_patient_outside_care_excluded
preconditions: Clinician A is authenticated with role:clinician
Patient X is assigned ONLY to Clinician B (not Clinician A)
Patient X's MRN is known and resolvable by the system
actions: GET /Patient?identifier=<patient_X_mrn> with Clinician A auth header
Inspect returned bundle entries for presence of Patient X
expected: HTTP 200 returned
Bundle contains zero entries (patient not in clinician's care)
AuditEvent still emitted (zero-result searches must be auditable)
No patient data from outside care scope is leaked in any field
trace: R-HLT-088: only patients in their care shall be returned
R-HLT-088: EP negative partition — out-of-care patient MRN search returns empty bundle
R-HLT-088: access logged for every operation including empty results
tests.executable:
def test_search_patient_outside_care_excluded(
    clinician_a_auth_headers, patient_outside_care, fhir_client, audit_poller
):
    mrn = patient_outside_care["identifier"][0]["value"]
    r = fhir_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"identifier": mrn},
        headers=clinician_a_auth_headers
    )
    assert r.status_code == 200
    bundle = r.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 0, "Out-of-care patient must not appear in results"
    audit = audit_poller("/AuditEvent", entity_type="Patient", action="E")
    assert len(audit) >= 1, "Audit must be emitted even for zero-result queries"

tests.name: test_search_patient_by_name_and_dob_combined
preconditions: Assigned patient with known family name AND known DOB exists
A second patient with same family name but different DOB also exists and is in clinician's care
actions: Verify only the patient matching BOTH criteria is returned
Confirm the same-name-different-DOB patient is absent from results
expected: HTTP 200 returned
Bundle entries contain exactly the patient matching both name AND DOB
Patient with same name but different DOB is NOT in results
AuditEvent emitted reflecting the combined search parameters
trace: R-HLT-088: clinician searches by name OR DOB (DT: both-param combination row)
tests.executable:
def test_search_patient_by_name_and_dob_combined(
    clinician_auth_headers, assigned_patient, same_name_different_dob_patient,
    fhir_client, audit_poller
):
    family = assigned_patient["name"][0]["family"]
    dob = assigned_patient["birthDate"]
    r = fhir_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"name": family, "birthdate": dob},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert r.status_code == 200
    bundle = r.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    ids = [e["resource"]["id"] for e in bundle.get("entry", [])]
    assert assigned_patient["id"] in ids, "Exact-match patient must be in results"
    assert same_name_different_dob_patient["id"] not in ids, "Different-DOB patient must be excluded"
    audit = audit_poller("/AuditEvent", entity_type="Patient", action="E")
    assert len(audit) >= 1
